title: NRO nominee says commercial space, AI are reshaping spy satellite agency
summary: Roger Mason highlighted the need for clear government demand signals as commercial space and AI transform the space intelligence market during a confirmation hearing. The NRO nominee emphasized how private investment is reshaping the spy satellite agency.

title: Global SSA Market to Reach $61B as Governments Prioritize Space Security, Resilience, and Orbital Safety
summary: The global Space Situational Awareness (SSA) market is projected to reach $61 billion as governments prioritize space security, resilience, and orbital safety. Novaspace's latest SSA report highlights a pivotal decade for increased spending in this sector.

title: The importance of determining an equilibrium state for space traffic management
summary: The rapid growth of human and robotic activity in low Earth orbit highlights the critical need for effective space traffic management (STM) to maintain safety and sustainability. Determining an equilibrium state for STM is essential to address this challenge.

title: UK explores Vast space station mission for astronaut with physical disability
summary: The UK government is considering sending British astronaut John McFall to Vast's Haven-1 space station, potentially making him the first person with a physical disability to live in orbit. This mission highlights efforts toward inclusivity in space exploration.

title: Impulse Space raises $500 million
summary: Impulse Space has secured $500 million in funding to scale up the production of orbital transfer vehicles and other spacecraft. The capital will help meet increasing commercial and government demand in the space industry.

title: Voyager to acquire lunar lander developer Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Technologies will acquire Astrobotic, a lunar lander and suborbital vehicle developer, in a deal valued up to $300 million.

title: Researchers call for regulations to protect low Earth orbit environment
summary: Researchers are urging for regulations to safeguard the low Earth orbit environment from the harmful effects of exotic materials released during satellite and space hardware re-entries. The call highlights the growing concern over atmospheric pollution caused by space debris and re-entry events.

title: NASA abandons ‘core module’ concept for commercial space station development
summary: NASA is abandoning its 'core module' concept for commercial space station development due to criticism from involved companies. The decision marks a shift in strategy for transitioning from the ISS to private orbital outposts.

title: France to fly two astronauts on Vast missions
summary: Vast has partnered with the French government to send two French astronauts on its missions, including the inaugural flight to its Haven-1 space station. This marks a significant step in commercial space station development and international collaboration.

title: Northrop Grumman partners with Apex on space-based interceptors for Golden Dome
summary: Northrop Grumman has partnered with Apex, a Los Angeles-based startup, to develop space-based interceptors for the Golden Dome program. Apex specializes in producing standardized, cost-effective satellite buses faster than traditional government spacecraft.

title: Scientists locate source of mysterious radio signals after 20 year search: A vampire star and its victim
summary: Scientists have identified the source of mysterious long-period radio signals after a 20-year search, revealing it to be a white dwarf star stealing matter from a nearby red dwarf star. The phenomenon occurs due to the clash of magnetic fields between the two stars.

title: China launches debut mission of Falcon 9-like rocket with no advance notice (video)
summary: China launched its first Long March 12B rocket, a partially reusable vehicle similar to SpaceX's Falcon 9, without prior airspace closure notices. The debut mission marks a significant step in China's reusable rocket development.

title: Casio's 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' watches will show you the time, as well as the way
summary: Casio has unveiled new G-Shock watches inspired by 'The Mandalorian and Grogu', blending Star Wars aesthetics with their signature timekeeping functionality. The collection highlights Casio's creative collaboration with the iconic franchise.

title: SpaceX wins $4 billion Space Force contract for satellites that target 'airborne threats' anywhere on Earth
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract to develop satellites for tracking airborne threats globally. This deal highlights SpaceX's expanding role in military space operations.

title: 'Total War: Warhammer 40,000': Release window, gameplay, factions & everything else we know about this upcoming grimdark sci-fi strategy game
summary: Creative Assembly is developing a new Total War game set in the Warhammer 40,000 universe, following the successful Total War: Warhammer trilogy.

title: 'Hot Jupiter' winds blasting at over 15,000 mph reveal 1st evidence of exoplanets with magnetic fields
summary: Astronomers found the first evidence of magnetic fields on exoplanets by observing violent winds exceeding 15,000 mph on 'Hot Jupiters'. This discovery marks a significant advancement in exoplanet research.

title: 10 easy places to see the total solar eclipse 2026 from Spain's major vacation destinations
summary: The article lists 10 locations in Spain, including urban beaches and mountain peaks, where the total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026, can be viewed. It focuses on popular vacation destinations along Spain's east coast and the Balearic Islands.

title: Celestron Origin Intelligent Home Observatory Mark II smart telescope review
summary: The Celestron Origin Mark II smart telescope allows users to capture high-quality images of deep-sky objects quickly after setup. It is designed for ease of use and advanced imaging capabilities.

title: Student-powered 'moon' rovers put to the test | Space photo of the day for June 2, 2026
summary: Hundreds of students designed, built, and tested rovers on an Earth obstacle course to prepare for lunar missions as part of a NASA challenge. The initiative aims to engage students in space technology development.

title: NASA Space Roboticist Challenge
summary: NASA's Fly Foundational Robots (FFR) mission will launch a seven-degree-of-freedom robotic arm to low Earth orbit, granting select U.S. researchers access to conduct experiments.

title: Look Up!
summary: ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot and NASA astronaut Jack Hathaway monitored the automated docking of a SpaceX Dragon cargo spacecraft to the ISS. The event occurred 259 miles above Earth during Expedition 74.

title: NASA Testing Wastewater Treatment Facility for Future Moon Base
summary: NASA has sent a mobile wastewater treatment system to the University of North Dakota for testing under conditions mimicking lunar and Martian environments. The technology aims to support long-duration missions on the Moon and Mars.

title: Be a Clump Scout and Help Reveal Secrets of Stellar Nurseries
summary: A citizen science project called 'Clump Scout' invites the public to help identify star-forming clumps in galaxy images, aiming to train machines for this task. The initiative leverages crowd participation to advance astronomical research.

title: Spacewalking With Scott Wray, Artemis EVA Training Lead
summary: Scott Wray, Artemis EVA Training Lead, shares his lifelong passion for spacewalks, beginning with imaginative play as a child. His early experiences laid the foundation for his current role in training astronauts for Artemis missions.

title: Fire’s Footprint on Santa Rosa Island
summary: A wildland fire burned across one-third of Santa Rosa Island, the second largest of the Channel Islands, affecting grassland, coastal sage scrub, and chaparral. The fire's footprint highlights ecological impacts on the island's diverse ecosystems.

title: NASA Awards Modification Contract for Reduced Gravity Test Aircraft
summary: NASA awarded a modification contract to Denmar Technical Services for aircraft modifications and maintenance for reduced gravity testing. The contract covers services for NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center and Johnson Space Center.

title: NASA Invites Media to See Roman Space Telescope Arrive at Kennedy
summary: NASA invites media to cover the arrival of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The telescope will be transported via NASA's Pegasus barge from Goddard Space Flight Center.

title: NASA to Conduct Low-Altitude Flights Near Houston
summary: NASA will conduct low-altitude research flights near Houston from June 3 to June 13 as part of the Student Airborne Research Program (SARP). Five aircraft will carry remote sensing payloads to support scientific missions over the Houston area.

title: What’s Up: June 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for June 2026, highlighting planetary alignments, lunar occultations, and the start of summer. The guide also points out deep-sky objects visible during the month.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has started recovery efforts at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week. Five missions are planned, with six launches scheduled in total.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into 'Webb’s Little Red Dots'. The discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a major anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, involving rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative includes rebranding existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo satellite launches dominating the manifest.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance activities throughout April. The ongoing operations highlight the station's role in supporting research and logistics in space.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to the Chinese Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 5, 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from SpaceX's facilities. The mission impressed observers with its performance during the inaugural flight.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned twice in the provided news snippet, but no specific details or context are given about its relevance to the space industry.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and select materials for spacecraft to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to improve mission success.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions and outreach.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology, likely in the space industry. However, the provided text lacks specific details or context.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a parachute system for a space mission, likely related to a Mars landing or high-altitude descent. The 'bake-out' process involves heating the parachute to remove contaminants and ensure proper deployment.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling secure docking and capture of objects in orbit. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to resume flight operations by the end of the year after assessing damage to its launch infrastructure. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the return-to-flight timeline on social media.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating the Space Development Agency (SDA) and the Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO) within the Pentagon, which are tasked with accelerating space acquisition. This move aims to streamline defense space programs but could disrupt ongoing efforts to modernize military space capabilities.

title: Northrop Grumman Taps Apex for Golden Dome SBI Collab
summary: Northrop Grumman has partnered with Apex to collaborate on the Golden Dome Space-Based Interceptor (SBI) for missile defense capabilities, with an on-orbit delivery target by 2027.

title: Voyager Will Acquire Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Space Holdings will acquire Astrobotic to expand its lunar infrastructure capabilities. The acquisition aims to support NASA's Artemis program by providing end-to-end lunar surface solutions.

title: Impulse Space Closes $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has closed a $500 million Series D funding round, continuing its current business trajectory without expanding into new ventures or facilities. The company's President and COO emphasized the need to maintain its existing growth path.

title: Bellatrix Aerospace Tapped to Build Korean VLEO Demo Sat
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace has partnered with Korean optical payload manufacturer TelePIX to build a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) demonstration satellite, targeting a launch no earlier than 2028.

title: Opinion: Commercial Space Is Generating Signals Decision-Makers Cannot Hear
summary: Commercial space activities are producing unprecedented operational signals, but decision-makers are struggling to process them due to a broken mechanism. The article highlights the disconnect between the volume of data generated and its effective communication to relevant authorities.

title: Nuggets in the SpaceX S-1 and Our Thoughts on the Business
summary: SpaceX's IPO prospectus has been released, revealing key insights about the company's business. The document highlights SpaceX's financial health and future plans.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Explodes on the Pad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket experienced a catastrophic failure during launch, resulting in an explosion on the pad. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's spaceflight ambitions.

title: Europe Celebrates its Sovereignty Progress at SmallSat
summary: Europe highlighted its progress in achieving orbital sovereignty at the SmallSat Europe event. The gathering emphasized the continent's advancements in independent space capabilities.

title: Impulse Space Raises Another Massive Funding Round in $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has raised $500 million in a Series D funding round led by 137 Ventures and Banner VC. This significant investment will support the company's growth in the space industry.

title: Iceye U.S. Appoints ASRC Federal Exec Ann Stevens as CEO
summary: Iceye U.S. has appointed Ann Stevens as CEO of its subsidiary, replacing Eric Jensen who now serves as COO. Stevens joins from ASRC Federal.

title: MDA Space to Supply Antennas to BAE Systems for Epoch 2 Missile Warning Constellation
summary: MDA Space has been selected by BAE Systems to supply antennas for the Epoch 2 missile warning constellation for the U.S. Space Force. The subcontract involves designing and delivering the antennas as part of the constellation's development.

title: Juniper Research Expects Massive Spike in Direct-to-Cell Subscribers
summary: Juniper Research predicts a significant increase in direct-to-cell (D2C) subscribers, with over 130 million active monthly users expected by 2031. The forecast is detailed in their report titled 'Direct to Cell Market 2026 – 2031.'

title: Are Orbital Data Centers the Next Frontier of AI Infrastructure?
summary: The space industry is exploring orbital data centers as a potential solution for AI infrastructure needs. Hyperscalers in cloud, AI, and space are competing to lead this emerging sector.

title: Sighting the Dark Fleet: Hyperspectral’s Role in Securing Safer Seas
summary: Iran closes the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation to U.S. and Israel strikes, escalating a regional war. Hyperspectral technology is highlighted for its role in securing maritime safety amid the conflict.

title: Orbital Data Centers: The Hype is There. Will the Reality Match it?
summary: The article discusses the hype surrounding orbital data centers in the satellite industry and questions whether the reality will live up to the expectations. It highlights the tendency of the space industry to generate excitement around emerging trends.

title: From Capacity to Capability: The New Playbook for Asia-Pacific Operators
summary: Regional satellite operators in the Asia-Pacific are shifting from traditional capacity-based models to capability-driven strategies due to the rise of megaconstellations and global consolidation. They are focusing on orchestration and integration to remain competitive.

title: How Can We Respond to the Skills Gap in Space?
summary: A 2023 Space Sector Skills Survey by the Space Skills Alliance reveals that 52% of U.K. space organizations face skills gaps in their workforce. The article discusses potential responses to this growing challenge.

title: The Earth Observation Market and Regulation: Instruments for Growth
summary: Satellite Earth Observation (EO) is highlighted as a key tool for Europe's defense, green transition, and digital transformation. The market and regulatory aspects of EO are discussed as instruments for growth.
